steps.title: Set up the cost formula
steps.content:
Robert pays 20 dollars as an initial fee, plus 0.70 dollars for each mile driven. For 38 miles, the total cost is:
$$\text{Total Cost} = 20 + 0.70 \times 38$$

steps.title: Calculate the total amount
steps.content:
Multiply the per-mile rate by the number of miles:
$$0.70 \times 38 = 26.6$$
Add this to the initial fee:
$$20 + 26.6 = 46.60$$
The total amount charged is 46.60 dollars. That matches Choice A — \$46.60.
